nl_proof: Explained:
Sentences used:
(1) The train leaves at noon.
(2) When does the train leave?
Knowledge used:
If some Y performs an event X and a situation Z is earlier than a situation W then Y performs X. Why: assumed basic knowledge.
If a situation X is followed by a situation Y then X is earlier than Y. Why: assumed basic knowledge.
The situation W0 is followed by the situation W1. Why: assumed basic knowledge.
Proof steps:
(1) If X happens some Z time some Y and X is a leave event and the train A performs X and X is an activity then [Z, Y] is the answer  [sentence 2]
(2) The event act4 is a leave event  [sentence 1]
(3) If the event act4 is an activity and the act4 happens some Y3 time some X3 and the train A performs the act4 then [Y3, X3] is the answer  [from steps 1, 2]
(4) The event act4 is an activity  [sentence 1]
(5) If the event act4 happens some Y time some X and the train A performs the act4 then [Y, X] is the answer  [from steps 3, 4]
(6) If some Y performs an event X and a situation Z is earlier than a situation W then Y performs X  [background knowledge]
(7) The train A performs the event act4  [sentence 1]
(8) If the situation W0 is earlier than a situation X then the train A performs the event act4  [from steps 6, 7]
(9) If a situation X is followed by a situation Y then X is earlier than Y  [background knowledge]
(10) The situation W0 is followed by the situation W1  [background knowledge]
(11) The situation W0 is earlier than the situation W1  [from steps 9, 10]
(12) The train A performs the event act4  [from steps 8, 11]
(13) If the event act4 happens some Y time some X then [Y, X] is the answer  [from steps 5, 12]
(14) The event act4 happens at noon  [sentence 1]
(15) 'At noon' is the answer  [from steps 13, 14]
